Document:
# Reptilian
[bold]Reptilians[/bold] are an [bold][color=#4df]Oxygen-breathing[/color][/bold], [bold][color=#f44]Iron-blooded[/color][/bold] species; covered in large scales, with long prehensile tails and big snouts.
Their big tails are strong enough to [bold]pull objects[/bold], keeping their hands free for other purposes.
Reptilians have the baseline body temperature of [bold][color=yellow]37°C[/color][/bold] (~[color=yellow]310°K[/color]). Due to their cold-blooded metabolism, reptilians experience the effects of [bold]hypothermia at [color=#4df]12°C[/color][/bold] and [bold]hyperthermia at [color=#f44]127°C[/color][/bold]. (~[color=#4df]285°K[/color] & [color=#f44]400°K[/color])
Reptilians are [bold]obligate carnivores[/bold], meaning they must eat [bold]meat-based foods[/bold], and [bold]can't stomach vegetarian meals[/bold], however they can [bold]safely digest raw meat and blood[/bold].
Their bodies can process [bold]2 reagents[/bold] at a time.
They slowly [bold]passively regenerate[/bold] all types of [bold][color=yellow]Brute[/color]damage [/bold], and individually [bold][color=#f44]Heat[/color]damage [/bold].
Reptilian given names typically follow a "[bold]Firstname-Lastname[/bold]" pattern, consisting frequently of tap [color=#999](/t, d/)[/color], trill [color=#999](/r/)[/color], fricative [color=#999](/s, z/)[/color], and nasal [color=#999](/m, n/)[/color] sounds.
Some Reptilians might adopt names in a "[bold]Verbs-the-Noun[/bold]" pattern, based on personal behavior or events they do frequently or take pride in.
